title: fix netmask
body:
* [x] Your changes are not possible to do through a plugin and relevant
    to a large audience (ideally all users of OctoPrint)
  * [x] If your changes are large or otherwise disruptive: You have
    made sure your changes don't interfere with current development by
    talking it through with the maintainers, e.g. through a
    Brainstorming ticket
  * [ ] Your PR targets OctoPrint's `devel` branch if it's a completely
    new feature, or `maintenance` if it's a bug fix or improvement of
    existing functionality for the current stable version (no PRs
    against `master` or anything else please)
  * [ ] Your PR was opened from a custom branch on your repository
    (no PRs from your version of `master`, `maintenance`, or `devel`
    please), e.g. `dev/my_new_feature` or `fix/my_bugfix`
  * [x] Your PR only contains relevant changes: no unrelated files,
    no dead code, ideally only one commit - rebase and squash your PR
    if necessary!
  * [x] Your changes follow the existing coding style
  * [ ] If your changes include style sheets: You have modified the
    `.less` source files, not the `.css` files (those are generated
    with `lessc`)
  * [x] You have tested your changes (please state how!) - ideally you
    have added unit tests
  * [ ] You have run the existing unit tests against your changes and
    nothing broke
  * [x] You have added yourself to the `AUTHORS.md` file :)


#### What does this PR do and why is it necessary?
in netifaces2 netmask was replaced with mask
#### How was it tested? How can it be tested by the reviewer?
manually modified in local deployment and ran OctoPrint
#### Any background context you want to provide?

#### What are the relevant tickets if any?
#5005
#### Screenshots (if appropriate)

#### Further notes

step_plan.summary: Make get_lan_ranges robust to netifaces renaming the mask key by accepting either 'netmask' or 'mask' when constructing netaddr.IPNetwork objects, restoring compatibility with netifaces2.

steps.operation: Accept both 'netmask' and 'mask' keys when building IPNetwork objects
steps.reason: netifaces historically returned per-interface netmask under the key 'netmask', but netifaces2 (or newer netifaces) uses 'mask'. get_lan_ranges currently only reads 'netmask' and will skip or mis-parse interfaces when the key is 'mask'. Updating the function to try address.get('netmask') and, if absent, address.get('mask') makes OctoPrint compatible with both variants while preserving existing IPv4/IPv6 parsing behavior.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral: Should have no API or public-behavior changes beyond restoring correct detection of local networks on systems where netifaces returns 'mask'. Tests: unit tests that assert get_lan_ranges output need to account for both key names; add/adjust tests to simulate address dicts with 'mask' to prevent regression. Dependents: discovery, SSDP, and any code that consumes get_lan_ranges results will regain correct network ranges — may change which interfaces are considered local on affected systems. Backward compatibility: maintains existing behavior for 'netmask' inputs. Risk: minimal, but ensure that parsing of prefix-length vs dotted masks (and IPv6 values that may include '/') remains unchanged; run existing test suite to confirm no regressions.
